beats.emotional_arc: Peaceful liberation and spiritual rebirth.
beats.pacing_notes: Slow, quiet, and resolute, contrasting sharply with the opening image.
beats.detailed_action: The ignited gas causes a massive explosion, blowing the monstrous mansion and Richard Sterling to kingdom come in a spectacular shower of debris. Valerie emerges from the smoldering crater, battered, bloody, but entirely alive and free from her debts. She spots a single, massive diamond resting in the ashes, picks it up for a fleeting moment, and then intentionally tosses it back into the rubble before walking away with Toby, finally ending the cycle of greed.

stage5_treatment.title_logline_characters:
TITLE: Open House
GENRE: Horror Comedy
LOGLINE: A ruthless real estate agent discovers her latest luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity that feeds on potential buyers, forcing her to market the deadly property to the worst people she knows to satisfy its hunger.
CHARACTERS:
VALERIE VANCE: A razor-sharp, morally bankrupt real estate agent drowning in secret debt.
RICHARD STERLING: The sociopathic, impeccably tailored head of Valerie's brokerage who views human beings strictly as capital.
TOBY ARIS: Valerie's chronically overworked, deeply empathetic assistant who dreams of building affordable housing.
JULIAN VANCE: A sleazy, flash-in-the-pan rival realtor who relies on nepotism and social media followers rather than skill.
COUNCILWOMAN ELENA ROSTOVA: A corrupt, untouchable city politician who zones low-income neighborhoods for luxury development.
stage5_treatment.act_1:
The morning sun offers no warmth to the manicured lawn of a modest suburban home, where VALERIE VANCE (34) stands like a perfectly tailored sentinel of doom. Impeccably dressed in a sharp designer suit, her face is a mask of cold detachment as she oversees the forced eviction of a struggling family. Movers ruthlessly haul cardboard boxes and worn furniture onto the curb, dismantling a life while Valerie checks her luxury watch, annoyed by the inefficiency of human despair.

The former homeowner, a tearful mother clutching a toddler, approaches Valerie in a desperate plea for just a few more days to gather their belongings. Valerie does not flinch, nor does she offer a single syllable of apology. Instead, her eyes remain dead and focused on the property's facade, calculating the exact cost of the cosmetic renovations required to flip the house.

Without a shred of empathy, Valerie aggressively pivots the conversation away from the woman's tears, launching into a rapid-fire lecture about market comps and property values. She coldly explains that this eviction is a necessary value add for the neighborhood's overarching financial health. In her mind, the interaction is purely transactional, cementing her deeply ingrained false belief that wealth is the absolute only shield against becoming a victim in a merciless world.

The sterile, glass-and-steel environment of the luxury brokerage serves as a stark contrast to the messy human emotion of the morning. Here, Valerie reigns supreme, striding past the desks of lesser agents while her chronically overworked assistant, TOBY ARIS (26), scurries to keep pace. Toby, deeply empathetic and visibly anxious, nervously adjusts his glasses and shuffles a stack of listing agreements, trying to physically organize his moral discomfort.

Unable to hold his tongue any longer, Toby timidly brings up the evicted family, stammering out a gentle suggestion that human beings possess an inherent value that extends far beyond their financial utility. He hopes to appeal to a shred of humanity buried beneath Valerie's polished exterior, desperately wanting to believe that the system they work for can be changed from the inside.

Valerie immediately shuts him down with a suffocating barrage of corporate buzzwords, instructing him to pivot his mindset and focus on their deliverables. She leans in close, her voice dropping to a sharp, venomous whisper as she delivers her ultimate thematic punchline: morality is a luxury that only the ultra-rich can afford. The exchange hangs in the cold air of the office, drawing a thick line in the sand between Toby's fragile idealism and Valerie's absolute moral bankruptcy.

The illusion of Valerie's untouchable success shatters when she is abruptly summoned into the cavernous, glass-walled office of her boss, RICHARD STERLING (55). Richard, a sociopathic apex predator in a bespoke suit, calmly pours himself a scotch while staring dead-eyed at the city skyline. He never raises his voice, speaking in measured, patronizing metaphors about market forces and the natural order of the food chain.

With chilling precision, Richard reveals that he is fully aware of Valerie's secret, mounting debts, noting that her financial instability has become a liability to his firm. He delivers a calm but devastating ultimatum: she is on the verge of termination, about to be cast out into the very poverty she has spent her entire life running from. Beneath her carefully composed veneer, Valerie experiences a surge of feral, panicked desperation, her greatest fear of powerlessness suddenly staring her in the face.

Cornered and fighting for her corporate survival, Valerie immediately goes on the offensive, targeting a sleazy, nepotism-fueled rival agent named JULIAN VANCE (32). Knowing Julian relies entirely on internet slang and unearned bravado to mask his incompetence, Valerie uncovers a scandalous secret about his fraudulent escrow accounts. She ruthlessly blackmails him in the agency breakroom, forcing him to hand over the firm's crown jewel: an exclusive listing for a bizarre, isolated modernist mansion, setting the stage for her descent into a literal nightmare.

The modernist mansion sits isolated against a backdrop of dense, shadowy woods, its sleek lines and cold glass exuding an aura of unnatural perfection. Valerie hosts a private, high-stakes showing for an obnoxiously wealthy tech bro, a client whose emotional abuse is as vast as his bank account. He struts through the pristine hallways, relentlessly insulting the property's architecture and threatening to ruin Valerie's career over the lack of smart-home integration.

Valerie maintains her plastic, professional smile, absorbing the client's toxic vitriol while mentally calculating the commission that will save her from ruin. The client stomps his designer shoes against the sleek hardwood floorboards, loudly declaring the house to be a hollow, worthless piece of garbage. The tension in the cavernous living room reaches a boiling point, the silence of the isolated property amplifying his cruel, echoing laughter.

Without a single warning, the physical reality of the room violently fractures. The sleek hardwood floorboards beneath the client's feet suddenly liquefy, peeling back to reveal a massive, fleshy maw lined with jagged, impossible teeth. In a gruesome, silent gulp, the house swallows the screaming billionaire whole, snapping its wooden jaws shut and instantly returning to a pristine, polished state. Valerie is thrown against the glass wall, paralyzed by absolute shock and primal terror as she realizes her luxury listing is a sentient, malevolent entity.

The deafening silence of the empty mansion rings in Valerie's ears, broken only by her own ragged, panicked breathing. Trembling violently, she scrambles backward away from the center of the room, her manicured hands fumbling to pull her cell phone from her designer blazer. Her thumb hovers agonizingly over the emergency dial pad, every instinct screaming at her to call the police and flee this architectural nightmare.

Before she can press the screen, the entire house emits a deep, guttural shudder that vibrates through the soles of her shoes. The floorboards in the center of the room ripple like water, and with a sickening, wet sound, the house regurgitates a single object onto the pristine wood. It lands with a heavy, crystalline clink, rolling to a stop just a few feet away from where Valerie cowers.

Valerie creeps forward, her breath catching in her throat as she stares down at a massive, flawless diamond, completely free of blood or biological residue. The ticking clock of her crushing financial ruin violently collides with the horrific reality of the murder she just witnessed. She stares at the gemstone, her mind racing as she weighs the absolute certainty of bankruptcy and homelessness against the dark, impossible wealth sitting right in front of her.

The harsh glow of the phone screen illuminates Valerie's conflicted face, the numbers 9-1-1 glaring back at her like a final moral test. The police are just one button press away, a choice that would end her career, expose her debts, and strip away her armor of wealth forever. But the diamond sparkles in the dim light of the mansion, whispering a promise of salvation, power, and the ultimate escape from victimization.

Slowly, deliberately, the feral panic drains from Valerie's eyes, replaced by a cold, calculating resolve. She lowers the phone, letting the screen fade to black, and drops to her knees on the very spot where the client was consumed. She reaches out, her fingers wrapping around the cold, heavy gemstone, and casually uses the sleeve of her expensive blazer to wipe a faint trace of ethereal saliva from its brilliant facets.

Valerie stands tall in the center of the silent, hungry room, the diamond clutched tightly in her fist like a weapon. She sweeps her gaze across the sleek, modernist walls, an unspoken, dark pact forging between the ruthless capitalist and the malevolent entity. She has actively chosen her path, fully embracing the sin of greed and stepping across the moral point of no return to become the caretaker of a monster.
stage5_treatment.act_2a:
Flush with untraceable cash from the liquidation of her first regurgitated diamond, Valerie pays off her crushing secret debts in a single, euphoric wire transfer. The suffocating weight of her financial ruin vanishes overnight, replaced by a dangerous, bulletproof arrogance. She struts into the sterile brokerage like a newly minted queen, pausing at Toby's cluttered desk to toss down a ridiculously expensive, transactional gift—a designer watch meant to buy his continued loyalty and mask her own lingering guilt.

Toby is absolutely thrilled by her sudden windfall, adjusting his glasses as his eyes shine with genuine, misplaced hope. Believing Valerie has finally secured the leverage needed to change the firm, he eagerly pitches his ultimate dream: a non-profit affordable housing trust funded by their luxury commissions. Valerie nods with a practiced, plastic smile, pretending to care about his noble crusade while a much darker realization dawns on her. Toby possesses an encyclopedic, heavily researched knowledge of the city's most corrupt, untouchable elites—the exact dietary requirements of her new architectural pet.

Under the guise of ambitious networking, Valerie manipulates Toby into curating a highly specific hit list of terrible clients. Toby eagerly complies, pulling up dossiers on slumlords and embezzlers, believing Valerie is finally using her influence to disrupt the corrupt system from the inside. He constantly reminds her of the human cost of their industry, acting as an unwitting moral anchor while Valerie secretly sizes up his targets for slaughter, perfectly blending her predatory intentions with corporate ambition.

A macabre, high-fashion slaughterhouse disguised as a luxury real estate campaign begins in earnest. Valerie hosts a series of exclusive, invite-only open houses, specifically targeting the monsters on Toby's meticulously researched list. COUNCILWOMAN ELENA ROSTOVA (50), dripping in stolen municipal funds and designer jewelry, sneers at the mansion's marble countertops before a pristine kitchen island suddenly splits open, dragging the corrupt politician screaming into a churning, fleshy garbage disposal of jagged drywall and rebar.

The carnage escalates into a darkly comedic rhythm of architectural horrors, transforming the modernist masterpiece into a shifting, deadly trap. A toxic crypto-billionaire is suffocated by a sentient, shrinking infinity pool; a predatory hedge fund manager is crushed flat by a rapidly descending vaulted ceiling that resets itself without a single drop of blood spilled. After each gruesome consumption, the house dutifully regurgitates a flawless, fist-sized diamond onto the immaculate floorboards, which Valerie collects with giddy, sociopathic triumph.

Valerie ascends to the absolute pinnacle of Richard Sterling's brokerage, her desk overflowing with congratulatory floral arrangements and vintage champagne. She completely ignores the mounting moral toll of her actions, intoxicated by the sheer power of weaponizing a malevolent entity for her own financial gain. The sudden disappearances of the city's worst elites are easily chalked up to spontaneous offshore vacations, leaving Valerie untouchable, wealthy beyond measure, and completely blind to the monster's rapidly expanding appetite.

Believing she has completely mastered the beast, Valerie revels in her lavish new lifestyle, sipping champagne while overseeing a routine, solitary cleaning of the modernist mansion. She treats the sentient entity like a tamed guard dog, a perfect, deadly machine that prints money on command. But as she casually wipes a speck of dust from a floating glass banister, a deep, unnatural groan vibrates through the foundation, instantly shattering her illusion of absolute control.

The pristine white walls suddenly crack like dry skin, seeping thick, black blood that ruins the imported Persian rugs. The house writhes and shudders, clearly unsatisfied with the meager, sporadic portions of single clients. With a violent, deafening slam, every heavy oak door and reinforced glass window in the mansion seals shut simultaneously, plunging the cavernous living room into terrifying, claustrophobic darkness.

Trapped and hyperventilating, Valerie pounds her manicured fists against the unbreakable glass, her arrogant control evaporating into primal, feral panic. The house violently shifts its floorboards, physically squeezing her toward the gaping, fleshy maw in the center of the room until she screams out a desperate, breathless bargain. She promises to organize a massive, invite-only gala, a veritable buffet of corporate scumbags to satisfy its ravenous hunger, realizing with chilling certainty that she is not the master of the house, but its captive pet.
stage5_treatment.act_2b:
The night of the gala arrives, transforming the sentient mansion into a glittering, high-stakes buffet. Valerie orchestrates the feeding with ruthless precision, packing the sprawling estate with the absolute worst of the city's elite, including RICHARD STERLING (55), her predatory, untouchable boss. Valerie plays the perfect hostess, her smile tight and her eyes darting nervously as she tries to guide the worst white-collar offenders toward the hungriest rooms. She glides through the crowd in a stunning crimson gown, a visual echo of the blood she is prepared to spill, desperately trying to manage the flow of the victims to appease the rumbling, impatient foundation beneath her designer stilettos.

The carefully curated feeding frenzy hits a massive snag in the form of JULIAN VANCE (38), a sleazy, cocaine-fueled rival realtor who has been tracking Valerie’s meteoric rise with venomous envy. Julian slips away from the champagne-swilling crowd, snooping through the restricted master suite and tearing through Valerie's hidden files. He corners her in a dimly lit hallway, brandishing a folder of missing persons reports and property anomalies, his breath reeking of expensive gin. He threatens to blow her entire empire wide open, demanding a massive cut of whatever illicit operation she is running out of the property.

But Julian’s blackmail is cut brutally short. The house, entirely devoid of patience for Valerie’s slow, methodical drip of victims, decides to take matters into its own hands. The sleek, architectural facade violently drops. The pristine white drywall suddenly ripples like bruised flesh, and the ambient recessed lighting flickers into a sickly, pulsating crimson. With a synchronized, ear-splitting slam, every single exit—from the grand mahogany double doors to the floor-to-ceiling patio glass—seals shut with thick, bone-like deadbolts. The mass consumption begins, trapping the city's most corrupt elite inside a literal, breathing death trap.

The modernist masterpiece instantly devolves into a chaotic, fleshy labyrinth of grinding teeth and crushing, shifting walls. Screams echo through the cavernous living room as a group of embezzling tech billionaires are swallowed whole by a suddenly liquefying sectional sofa. Valerie navigates the blood-soaked carnage with a terrifying, bulletproof arrogance, assuming her status as the house's provider grants her absolute immunity. She strides through the shifting corridors, dodging snapping floorboards, fully expecting the entity to gorge itself on the corrupt elites and leave her to collect the regurgitated diamonds in peace.

Her sociopathic confidence shatters into absolute horror when the house abruptly changes its dietary preferences. Bypassing a cowering Richard Sterling, the entity’s fleshy tendrils whip across the room, snatching up the terrified, underpaid catering staff. The innocent servers are dragged screaming into the ceiling vents, their silver trays of hors d'oeuvres clattering against the blood-stained marble. The monster is no longer a surgical instrument of vigilante justice; it is a gluttonous, indiscriminate beast, and its hunger has mutated far beyond the sinful.

The true, devastating cost of Valerie's sin crystallizes when the house sets its sights on TOBY (26). Her fiercely loyal assistant, who spent the evening naively pitching his affordable housing trust to the trapped billionaires, is suddenly cornered by a rapidly narrowing hallway of jagged, calcified drywall. Valerie watches the walls close in on the only decent person in her life, the crushing weight of her unmitigated greed finally suffocating her ego. She experiences the undeniable, putrid whiff of death, realizing her ambition has unleashed an apocalyptic force that is about to slaughter the very innocence she used as a moral shield.

Frantic and stripped of all her glamorous armor, Valerie dives through the shifting, fleshy corridors, grabbing Toby by the collar just as the walls attempt to crush him flat. She drags him toward the grand floating staircase, but the house anticipates her move. The glass steps violently buckle and collapse, trapping them on a narrow, trembling landing. Beneath them, the polished hardwood splits wide open, revealing a gaping, acidic maw lined with rows of translucent, jagged teeth. The stench of sulfur and digested greed billows up from the pit, choking them both as the house corners its provider.

A deep, guttural vibration rattles through Valerie’s skull, a telepathic demand from the entity that bypasses her ears and strikes directly at her soul. The house offers her a sickeningly simple ultimatum: push the innocent, terrified assistant into the churning acid below, and she can walk out the front door with her life, her wealth, and their lucrative partnership intact. The entity pulses with a dark, seductive energy, promising her the absolute pinnacle of the real estate world if she just sheds this final ounce of dead weight.

Valerie stares into Toby’s wide, tear-filled eyes, his hands trembling as he clings to her crimson gown for salvation. In his terrified reflection, she sees the horrifying truth of her own identity. She is forced into an agonizing, intimate self-reflection amidst the deafening screams of the dying gala. The psychological wound of her past financial ruin, the desperation that drove her to this madness, suddenly feels utterly hollow. She realizes with absolute, despairing clarity that her relentless pursuit of wealth has not saved her; it has simply transformed her into the exact same predatory, soul-sucking monster as Richard Sterling.
stage5_treatment.act_3:
The guttural vibration of the entity's ultimatum still rattles through VALERIE (34) as she stares down into the acidic, tooth-lined chasm. The house pulses with a sickly, seductive warmth, promising her an empire of regurgitated diamonds if she just lets go of TOBY (26). Instead, a fierce, righteous defiance ignites within her chest. She violently yanks Toby backward by the lapels of his cheap suit, hauling him away from the snapping, translucent teeth. The house shrieks in response, a deafening wail of twisting metal and tearing drywall that shakes the very foundation. Valerie stands tall against the shifting architecture, shouting into the cavernous maw that she is breaking the lease, permanently severing her symbiotic pact with the gluttonous beast.

The mansion immediately retaliates, transforming from a passive trap into an active predator. The grand staircase fully collapses into a slide of jagged glass and splintered wood, but Valerie’s intimate, obsessive knowledge of the floor plan becomes her ultimate weapon. She grabs Toby’s hand and sprints through the collapsing foyer, dodging snapping floorboards and raining chandeliers. She remembers the original blueprints, specifically the structural vulnerabilities she once exploited to hide the mansion's grisly secret. She leads Toby toward the servant's corridors, a narrow labyrinth the entity has largely ignored in its feast.

Along the way, they stumble upon a cluster of terrified, blood-spattered catering staff huddled behind a shattered marble island in the kitchen. The walls around them are beginning to secrete a corrosive, digestive acid, slowly melting the stainless steel appliances. Valerie takes absolute command, her previous sociopathic arrogance replaced by a desperate, protective fury. She herds the surviving innocents through the melting pantry, kicking down a reinforced door that leads down into the subterranean mechanical room. The entity roars, sending fleshy tendrils whipping down the stairwell, but Valerie manages to slam the heavy steel door shut, buying them a temporary reprieve in the damp, vibrating basement.

In the dim, flickering light of the mechanical room, Valerie quickly directs the weeping staff toward an old, rusted ventilation shaft that leads directly to the exterior hillside. Toby helps hoist the servers into the narrow escape route, the sounds of the house tearing itself apart echoing from above. Just as Valerie turns to follow them, a heavy iron pipe swings out from the shadows, smashing into her ribs. She collapses to the concrete floor, gasping for air, as RICHARD STERLING (55) steps into the dim light. Her predatory boss is covered in the blood of his colleagues, his expensive tuxedo torn, but his eyes are wide with a manic, terrifying greed. He has deduced the nature of the house and the source of Valerie's sudden wealth, and he has zero intention of letting the golden goose die.

Richard declares that the property is too valuable to abandon, gleefully attempting to drag Valerie back toward the basement door to offer her up as a peace offering to the hungry architecture. A brutal, bloody fistfight erupts in the cramped, pulsating mechanical room. Valerie fights with the feral desperation of a woman who has already lost her soul and is clawing it back inch by inch. She dodges Richard’s wild swings, driving her knee into his stomach and slamming his head against the vibrating water heater. The house, sensing the conflict, begins to shrink the room, the concrete walls literally closing in to crush them both into a fine paste.

The space grows claustrophobically tight, the grinding of the shifting foundation threatening to deafen them. Richard tackles Valerie, his hands wrapping tightly around her throat as he tries to choke the life out of her, screaming that the real estate market belongs to the ruthless. Valerie’s vision blurs, but her flailing hand finds a heavy, cast-iron wrench abandoned on the floor. With a final surge of adrenaline, she brings the wrench crashing down onto Richard’s skull, shattering his grip. She scrambles away from his bleeding, dazed form, her eyes locking onto the mansion's massive, industrial gas main.

Knowing the entity is closing in for the final kill, Valerie swings the wrench with all her might, smashing the main gas line valve. A deafening hiss fills the shrinking room as thick, volatile fumes pour into the enclosed space. Richard stumbles to his feet, coughing and screaming in protest as the fleshy, tooth-lined walls finally burst through the concrete, lunging toward them. Valerie pulls a silver lighter from her crimson gown, sparks the flint, and tosses it directly into the geyser of gas. Without looking back, she dives headfirst into the mansion's reinforced, titanium-lined panic room, slamming the heavy vault door shut just as the entity's maw snaps shut on Richard Sterling.

The ignited gas triggers a catastrophic, earth-shattering explosion that rips through the subterranean levels and tears the modernist masterpiece apart from the inside out. The blast wave obliterates the fleshy labyrinth, incinerating the grinding teeth, the shifting walls, and the manic Richard Sterling in a spectacular, cleansing firestorm. The detonation blows the roof clean off the foundation, sending a shower of burning debris, shattered glass, and pulverized drywall raining down across the pristine Hollywood Hills. Inside the panic room, Valerie is thrown violently against the titanium walls, the world outside erupting into a blinding, roaring inferno that finally silences the demonic heartbeat of the house.

Hours later, the smoke begins to clear, revealing a smoldering, blackened crater where the architectural marvel once stood. The heavy vault door of the panic room groans open, and Valerie emerges into the cool morning air. She is battered, covered in soot, and her stunning crimson gown is reduced to blood-stained rags, but she is entirely alive. The oppressive, suffocating weight of her debts and her ambition has vanished, replaced by a profound, peaceful liberation. She climbs out of the wreckage, her lungs filling with the crisp, clean air of a new dawn, completely free from the monstrous cycle she had perpetuated.

As she navigates the charred rubble, a glint of light catches her eye. Resting perfectly intact amidst the gray ashes is a single, massive diamond, a final, tempting regurgitation from the destroyed beast. Valerie pauses, reaching down to pick up the flawless stone. She rolls it between her fingers for a fleeting moment, feeling its cold, heavy promise of infinite wealth. Then, with a quiet, resolute exhale, she intentionally tosses the diamond back into the smoldering ruins. She turns her back on the fortune, walking away from the crater to join Toby, who waits for her on the distant hillside, ready to rebuild her life from the ground up.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODEST SUBURBAN HOME - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Movers ruthlessly haul worn furniture onto the curb. Valerie Vance stands perfectly still on the manicured lawn, impeccably dressed. A tearful mother clutching a toddler approaches, pleading for more time. Valerie does not flinch. She checks her luxury watch, turns her back on the crying woman, and visually inspects the property's facade. She cuts off the mother's pleas with a rapid-fire lecture about market comps and neighborhood value adds, physically blocking the woman from re-entering the house.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Opening Image: Establishes Valerie's profound moral bankruptcy, her transactional view of humanity, and her deeply ingrained false belief that wealth is the only shield against victimization.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - BULLPEN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie strides aggressively through the sterile, glass-and-steel bullpen. Toby Aris, her anxious assistant, scurries behind her, nervously adjusting his glasses and shuffling a messy stack of listing agreements. Toby stops in the middle of the aisle, forcing Valerie to halt, and timidly suggests the evicted family deserved basic empathy. Valerie steps directly into Toby's personal space, unleashes a barrage of corporate buzzwords, and drops her voice to a venomous whisper to declare that morality is a luxury only the ultra-rich can afford. She turns on her heel, leaving Toby stunned.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Theme Stated: Explicitly contrasts Toby's fragile idealism against Valerie's ruthless capitalism, posing the central thematic question of human value versus financial utility.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. RICHARD STERLING'S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie stands rigidly inside a cavernous, glass-walled office. Richard Sterling stands by the window with his back to her, calmly pouring a glass of scotch. He speaks in measured, patronizing tones, detailing his full awareness of Valerie's secret, mounting debts. Valerie's polished veneer cracks; her breathing quickens and her knuckles turn white as she grips the back of a leather guest chair. Richard turns, locking her in a dead-eyed stare, and delivers a quiet ultimatum: she is a liability on the verge of termination. Valerie swallows hard, her eyes darting in feral panic, and exits without a word.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Inciting Incident / Setup: Strips away Valerie's armor of success, exposing her deepest fear of poverty and forcing her into a desperate corner where she must take drastic action.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. LUXURY BROKERAGE - BREAKROOM - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie bursts into the breakroom, moving with the frantic energy of a cornered animal. She spots Julian Vance loudly bragging into his phone. Valerie marches over, snatches the phone from his hand, and slams the door shut. She slaps a manila folder onto the counter, exposing printouts of Julian's fraudulent escrow accounts. Julian's arrogant smile vanishes; he aggressively grabs for his phone to avoid eye contact. Valerie blocks his reach, demanding the physical keys to his exclusive modernist mansion listing. Defeated, Julian trembling drops the heavy brass keys into her open palm.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Takes Action: Demonstrates Valerie's cutthroat resourcefulness under pressure and actively secures the specific location where the supernatural elements will emerge.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: A sleek, cold glass-and-steel mansion sits unnaturally perfect against a backdrop of dense, shadowy woods. Valerie's luxury sedan parks on the sweeping driveway. A loud, obnoxious sports car aggressively pulls up right behind her. A wealthy Tech Bro steps out, immediately shouting into his phone about the lack of cell service. Valerie takes a deep breath, pastes on a plastic, professional smile, and unlocks the massive front door, gesturing for the hostile client to step inside.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Atmosphere & Transition: Establishes the isolation and eerie perfection of the mansion, physically cutting the protagonist and her prey off from the outside world.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER / HALLWAYS - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie leads the Tech Bro through pristine, echoing hallways. The Tech Bro struts arrogantly, trailing his fingers along the walls and relentlessly mocking the minimalist architecture. Valerie absorbs the toxic vitriol, her eyes tracking his movements while she mentally calculates her commission. The Tech Bro stops, jabbing a finger inches from Valerie's face, threatening to ruin her career over the lack of smart-home integration. Valerie physically steps back, hands clasped tightly in front of her, maintaining her forced smile despite the escalating abuse.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalation: Builds tension through the client's obnoxious behavior, making the audience resent him while highlighting Valerie's desperate need to close the deal at any cost.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: They enter a cavernous living room. The Tech Bro loudly declares the house a hollow piece of garbage and aggressively stomps his designer shoes against the sleek hardwood floor. Without warning, the floorboards beneath him liquefy, peeling back to reveal a massive, fleshy maw lined with jagged teeth. The house swallows the screaming billionaire whole in a single, silent gulp. The wooden floor snaps shut, instantly returning to a pristine state. The shockwave of the floor snapping shut throws Valerie backward. She slams against a glass wall and slides to the floor, paralyzed.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Turn / Supernatural Reveal: Shatters the grounded reality of the film, introducing the Monster and delivering the first major genre thrill of the narrative.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Deafening silence fills the room. Valerie cowers against the glass, hyperventilating. She scrambles backward on her hands and knees, terrified the floor will open beneath her. Fumbling frantically in her designer blazer, she yanks out her cell phone. Her trembling thumb hovers over the emergency dial pad. She punches in 9-1-1. Her finger rests a millimeter above the call button, her entire body shaking as she fights the instinct to flee.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Reaction: Grounds the horror in a realistic, panicked human response, setting up the immediate moral crossroads Valerie is about to face.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: A deep, guttural shudder vibrates through the floorboards, knocking Valerie off balance. The center of the room ripples like water. With a sickening, wet squelch, the house regurgitates a single, heavy object. It hits the pristine wood with a crystalline clink and rolls to a stop near Valerie's feet. She creeps forward, breath hitched, and stares down at a massive, flawless diamond, completely free of blood or biological residue.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Complication: Introduces the supernatural reward mechanism, instantly complicating Valerie's decision to call the authorities and weaponizing her deepest desires.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The harsh glow of the 9-1-1 screen illuminates Valerie's conflicted face. She looks from the phone to the sparkling diamond. The feral panic in her eyes slowly hardens into a cold, calculating resolve. She lowers the phone, her thumb deleting the numbers until the screen goes black. Dropping to her knees, she grabs the cold gemstone, using the sleeve of her expensive blazer to wipe off a faint trace of ethereal saliva. Valerie stands tall in the center of the hungry room, clutching the diamond like a weapon, her posture entirely composed.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Sequence Climax / The Choice: Valerie actively crosses the moral point of no return. She chooses wealth over humanity, sealing her dark pact with the entity and launching Act II.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's luxury sedan winds up a deeply shadowed, forested driveway, breaking through the tree line to reveal a brutalist, glass-and-steel mansion. The isolation is absolute; not even birds are chirping. Valerie steps out, her heels clicking sharply against the concrete. She retrieves a heavy brass key from her designer bag, marches to the oversized front door, and shoves the key into the lock with forceful, desperate precision.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Atmosphere & Setting: Establishes the extreme isolation of the property, physically cutting Valerie and her future prey off from the outside world.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER / KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie moves through the cavernous, echoing house, aggressively turning on smart-lights and adjusting staging furniture. The air inside is unnaturally cold. As she straightens a stack of architectural magazines on the marble kitchen island, a heavy mahogany door down the hall slowly swings shut on its own with a soft click. Valerie pauses, her eyes darting toward the sound, but she shakes her head, dismisses the creepiness, and checks her luxury watch.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Tension Building: Introduces subtle, supernatural 'wrongness' to the house, highlighting Valerie's willingness to ignore red flags in pursuit of a paycheck.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DRIVEWAY - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: A violently loud, neon-green sports car screeches to a halt right behind Valerie's sedan. A wealthy TECH BRO (30s, wearing a tech-fleece vest and designer sunglasses) steps out, screaming into his cell phone about firing a subordinate. He casually tosses a half-empty plastic green juice bottle onto the pristine manicured lawn. Valerie takes a deep breath, pastes on a flawless, plastic smile, and walks out to greet him, completely ignoring the litter.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Character Introduction: Introduces the victim, establishing him as instantly abhorrent and morally bankrupt, making the audience subconsciously root for his demise.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - KITCHEN / HALLWAY - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie leads the Tech Bro through the kitchen. He immediately taps his knuckles aggressively against the countertops, mocking the minimalist aesthetic as 'sterile garbage.' Valerie absorbs the insult, her eyes briefly dropping to his $100,000 wristwatch. She smoothly deflects, physically maneuvering herself between him and a minor scuff on the wall, rapid-firing market comps to justify the asking price.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalation: The client's toxic behavior tests Valerie's polished veneer, visually demonstrating the emotional abuse she endures to survive financially.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - MASTER BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: The Tech Bro realizes he has no cell service. He snaps. He steps aggressively into Valerie's personal space, jabbing a finger inches from her nose, threatening to blacklist her across the entire city if the house isn't wired with fiber optics. Valerie's fake smile strains; her knuckles turn white as she grips her clipboard. A low, barely audible hum vibrates through the floorboards beneath them, as if the house is reacting to his raised voice.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Rising Action: Pushes Valerie to her breaking point while subtly hinting that the house is 'listening' and reacting to the hostility in the room.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: They enter the massive, glass-walled living room. The Tech Bro spins around in the center of the room, loudly declaring the property a hollow, overpriced joke. To emphasize his disgust, he violently stomps his heavy designer boots against the sleek hardwood floor. Valerie physically recoils from the noise, her patience finally snapping as she opens her mouth to snap back at him.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Trigger: The client's physical abuse of the property serves as the final catalyst, pushing the tension to its absolute peak right before the supernatural reveal.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Without warning, the floorboards beneath the Tech Bro liquefy. The wood peels back like flesh, revealing a massive, cavernous maw lined with jagged, impossible teeth. In a single, silent, hyper-violent gulp, the house swallows the screaming billionaire whole. The wooden jaws snap shut with a deafening CRACK, instantly returning to a pristine, polished state. The shockwave blasts Valerie off her feet. She slams hard against the glass wall and slides to the floor, eyes wide in absolute terror.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Catalyst / Supernatural Reveal: Shatters the grounded corporate reality of the film. The Monster is revealed, completely upending Valerie's world.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: Deafening silence. Valerie hyperventilates, her chest heaving. She scrambles backward on her hands and knees, treating the hardwood floor like thin ice. Fumbling frantically inside her designer blazer, she yanks out her cell phone. Her trembling thumb punches in 9-1-1. She hovers her finger a millimeter above the green call button, her entire body shaking as she prepares to report the impossible murder.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Reaction: Grounds the horror in a realistic, primal human response. Valerie's instinct is to flee and seek help, setting up the impending moral dilemma.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Before she can press call, a deep, guttural shudder vibrates through the room, knocking her phone sideways. The center floorboards ripple like water. With a sickening, wet squelch, the house regurgitates a heavy object. It hits the wood with a crystalline clink and rolls directly to Valerie's knees. She stops breathing. She leans in, staring down at a massive, flawless diamond, completely devoid of blood.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Complication: Introduces the supernatural reward mechanism. The house weaponizes Valerie's deepest desire (wealth) to stop her from calling the authorities.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The harsh glow of the 9-1-1 screen illuminates Valerie's terrified face. She looks from the phone to the sparkling diamond. She squeezes her eyes shut, and her hand moves instinctively to her empty, maxed-out designer wallet in her pocket. The feral panic in her eyes slowly drains away, replaced by a cold, calculating stillness. She stares at the spot where the man was eaten, her breathing slowing to a calm, steady rhythm.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Debate: The internal moral crossroads. Valerie actively weighs her crushing financial ruin against the horrific reality of becoming an accomplice to murder.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie lowers the phone. Her thumb deliberately deletes the numbers 9-1-1, and the screen goes black. She drops to her knees, reaches out, and grabs the cold gemstone. Without hesitating, she uses the sleeve of her $2,000 blazer to wipe a faint trace of ethereal saliva from the diamond's facets. She stands up tall in the center of the hungry room, slipping the diamond into her pocket. She nods once to the empty room.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Break into Two: Valerie crosses the moral point of no return. She chooses the sin of greed over her humanity, sealing her dark pact with the entity.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie exits the front door, her posture impeccably straight and composed. She locks the heavy deadbolt with a definitive click. She turns and looks at the Tech Bro's neon-green sports car still idling in the driveway. Without a shred of emotion, she steps around the litter on the lawn, gets into her sedan, and drives away down the shadowed forest road, leaving the car and the victim behind.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Resolution of the Sequence: Visual confirmation of her complicity. She leaves the scene of the crime, fully locked into her new role as the monster's caretaker.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. BROKERAGE FIRM - BULLPEN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie strides through the glass-walled brokerage in a razor-sharp, impossibly expensive new designer suit. She stares at her phone screen, where a banking app displays a suffocating red balance of '-$842,500.' Her thumb hovers, then aggressively taps 'TRANSFER.' The screen flashes green: '$0.00.' Valerie exhales a breath she's been holding for years, her posture instantly shifting from rigid survival to predatory arrogance. She tosses her empty coffee cup into a bin without breaking stride.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The B-Story Catalyst: Establishes Valerie's newfound financial invincibility and sets her up to interact with Toby from a position of absolute power.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. BROKERAGE FIRM - TOBY'S DESK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drops a heavy velvet box onto Toby's cluttered desk. He opens it to find a $20,000 Rolex. Toby stammers, nervously adjusting his glasses and shuffling his affordable housing proposals. Valerie leans in, tapping a manicured nail on his meticulously researched 'Corrupt Landlords & Officials' dossier. Toby eagerly flips the binder open, pointing to Councilwoman Rostova's profile. Valerie's eyes lock onto Rostova's photo, a predatory smile stretching across her face as she slides the file toward herself.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Preparation / Manipulation: Valerie weaponizes Toby's empathy, securing her hit list of victims while masking her murderous intent as ambitious corporate disruption.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - KITCHEN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Councilwoman Rostova, dripping in gold jewelry, aggressively taps her designer heels against the floor while sneering at the kitchen's minimalist aesthetic. Valerie stands safely by the doorway, hands clasped behind her back. Rostova leans her weight against the pristine marble island. The marble suddenly softens like warm wax. With a wet tear, the island splits down the middle, revealing a churning vortex of jagged rebar and drywall. Rostova is violently yanked waist-deep into the architectural garbage disposal.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalation (Fun & Games): The first targeted feeding. Delivers on the premise by showing the house creatively devouring a specifically chosen, morally bankrupt victim.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - KITCHEN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.narrative_action: The island snaps shut, returning to flawless marble. A sickening gulp echoes through the plumbing. From the gleaming chrome sink faucet, a fist-sized, blood-free diamond drops with a heavy CLINK into the stainless steel basin. Valerie casually walks over, plucks the diamond from the sink, and uses a thick black marker to violently cross Rostova's name off Toby's dossier.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Reward Mechanism: Cements the transactional nature of Valerie's pact with the house. Murder equals immediate, pristine wealth.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - INFINITY POOL - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: A toxic Crypto-Billionaire swims aggressive laps in the glass-edged infinity pool. Valerie sits on a distant lounger, scrolling on her phone. Slowly, the concrete edges of the pool begin to slide inward. The billionaire stops, treading water in confusion as the pool rapidly shrinks. He thrashes, screaming for help as the glass walls close in, compressing the water into a violent, churning geyser. The pool seals shut into a solid concrete patio, leaving only a sparkling diamond resting in the center drain.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Montage Beat 1: Accelerates the pacing and body count, showcasing the house's shifting, deadly architecture and Valerie's growing desensitization.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: A predatory Hedge Fund Manager paces the living room, loudly complaining on his cell phone. Valerie checks her watch, takes a deliberate step backward out of the center of the room, and sips a glass of champagne. Above the manager, the massive vaulted ceiling silently detaches. It plummets like a hydraulic press, flattening the man instantly against the floorboards. The ceiling seamlessly retracts. Valerie calmly walks over and catches the regurgitated diamond before it hits the ground.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Montage Beat 2: Completes the 'Fun and Games' sequence, demonstrating Valerie's absolute comfort and sociopathic mastery over the slaughter process.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. BROKERAGE FIRM - VALERIE'S CORNER OFFICE - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie sits behind a massive mahogany desk in a sprawling corner office overflowing with lavish floral arrangements. Richard Sterling enters, his dead-eyed sociopathic stare replaced by a rare, approving nod. He silently places a bottle of vintage Dom Pérignon on her desk—an acknowledgment of her apex status. Outside the glass walls, Toby watches nervously, clutching a massive donation check Valerie just signed, his moral compass visibly spinning.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: False Victory: Valerie achieves her external goal (wealth and status at the top of the food chain), creating a peak of arrogance right before the fall.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - STAIRCASE / LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie, dressed in casual cashmere, hums a cheerful tune while running a microfiber cloth over the floating glass banister. She treats the house with the affectionate arrogance of a wealthy dog owner grooming a prize-winning pitbull. As she wipes away a single speck of dust, a deep, bass-heavy groan vibrates through the soles of her shoes, rattling the glass in her hand.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Turn: Shifts the power dynamic. Valerie's illusion of control is challenged by the house's physical unrest.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie freezes. Hairline fractures begin to spiderweb across the pristine white drywall of the living room. With a sickening squelch, the cracks widen, and thick, black, viscous blood begins to seep from the walls, dripping heavily onto the imported Persian rugs. Valerie drops her microfiber cloth, her polished demeanor cracking as she takes a slow, terrified step backward.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalation of Threat: The house physically corrupts its own pristine aesthetic to communicate its insatiable, growing hunger.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: BAM. Every heavy oak door in the hallway slams shut simultaneously. The reinforced glass windows lock with deafening, synchronized metallic CLACKS. The smart-lights flicker and die, plunging the cavernous room into deep shadows. Valerie lunges for the front door, grabbing the brass handle and yanking with all her body weight, but the mechanism is fused solid.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Trap: Physically isolates Valerie, stripping away her escape routes and forcing a direct confrontation with the monster.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The floorboards groan and violently buckle, tilting upward at a steep forty-five-degree angle. Valerie loses her footing and slides down the slick hardwood. At the center of the room, the floor peels back to reveal the massive, fleshy maw, its jagged wooden teeth gnashing eagerly. Valerie claws frantically at the floorboards, her expensive manicured nails breaking and bleeding as she fights gravity, her polished veneer entirely replaced by feral, screaming panic.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Climax of the Sequence: The physical manifestation of Valerie's worst fear—being powerless and consumed by the very system she thought she mastered.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Dangling inches above the snapping jaws, Valerie screams out a desperate, breathless bargain to the empty room. She promises a gala—a massive, invite-only buffet of the city's worst elites. The gnashing teeth freeze. The floorboards slowly, mechanically lower back to a perfectly level, pristine state. The front door unlocks with a soft click. Valerie collapses onto her back, chest heaving, staring up at the ceiling as she realizes she is merely the monster's captive pet.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Sequence Resolution / New Goal: Valerie survives by making a massive, impossible promise, setting up the ticking clock and the high-stakes gala for Act II's climax.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. BROKERAGE FIRM - BULLPEN - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie strides into the sterile brokerage wearing a razor-sharp, impossibly expensive new designer suit. She moves with bulletproof arrogance. Julian Vance steps into her path, smirking and dropping internet slang about her 'lucky vibes' with the mansion. Valerie doesn't blink. She loudly lists Julian's declining market comps to the entire bullpen and casually threatens to absorb his desk space. Julian's smile tightens; he aggressively checks his phone to avoid eye contact and steps aside.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes Valerie's false victory and unchecked arrogance at the top of the food chain, highlighting her dominance over her rivals.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. BROKERAGE FIRM - RICHARD'S OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Richard Sterling, impeccably tailored, watches Valerie enter. He doesn't speak, simply pouring two glasses of vintage Macallan. He hands her one, delivering a calm, patronizing metaphor about apex predators and the natural order of the market. He clinks his glass against hers, officially welcoming her to the inner circle. Valerie drinks it in, her desire for absolute wealth fully realized.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Cements Valerie's alliance with the primary antagonist and reinforces the core theme of corporate greed being rewarded.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. BROKERAGE FIRM - TOBY'S DESK - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drops a massive stack of signed closing documents on Toby's desk. Toby nervously adjusts his glasses, whispering that the police are looking into the sudden disappearances of Councilwoman Rostova and the hedge fund manager. Valerie deflects instantly, pointing to Toby's affordable housing blueprints and telling him to focus on 'the value add.' She announces she's going to the property for a routine walkthrough and leaves Toby stewing in moral discomfort.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Introduces the ticking clock of the police investigation and isolates Valerie from her moral anchor before she heads to the house.

scenes.scene_heading: EXT. MODERNIST MANSION - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie's brand-new matte-black Porsche pulls up the sweeping driveway. She steps out, inhaling the crisp air, and looks up at the towering, geometric facade of the mansion. She smirks, patting the exterior concrete wall affectionately as if praising a loyal guard dog, completely blind to the slight, unnatural tremor in the stone beneath her hand.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Transitions Valerie into the belly of the beast, emphasizing her delusion of control over the malevolent entity.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - DAY
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie kicks off her designer heels and wanders the cavernous, sunlit living room with a glass of champagne. She spots a single smudge on the floating glass banister. Setting her glass down, she pulls a microfiber cloth from a drawer and casually wipes the glass, humming to herself. She speaks aloud to the empty room, promising it a 'light snack' next week.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The calm before the storm. Lowers the tension to make the impending attack more jarring, showcasing her hubris.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: A deep, bass-heavy groan vibrates through the soles of Valerie's bare feet. The champagne in her glass ripples violently. She freezes. Hairline fractures suddenly spiderweb across the pristine white drywall. With a sickening squelch, the cracks widen, and thick, black, viscous blood begins to seep from the walls, dripping heavily onto the imported Persian rugs.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Turn / Midpoint Catalyst: The house physically corrupts its own aesthetic, shattering Valerie's illusion of mastery.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - FOYER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drops the cloth, her polished veneer cracking. She speed-walks toward the heavy oak front door, trying to maintain her composure. Just as her hand grazes the brass handle, BAM. Every door in the hallway slams shut simultaneously. The deadbolts slide into place on their own. Valerie yanks the handle with all her body weight, but the mechanism is fused solid.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Escalation: Physically traps the protagonist, stripping away her escape routes and spiking the primal fear.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie sprints into the kitchen, aiming for the glass patio doors. The smart-blinds violently snap shut. The marble island in the center of the room begins to rumble, softening and tearing open. The garbage disposal roars to life like a jet turbine, creating a localized vacuum that pulls violently at Valerie's clothes. She screams, scrambling backward out of the kitchen.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Denies the secondary exit and reminds Valerie (and the audience) of the brutal fate that awaits her.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie retreats to the center of the living room. The smart-lights flicker red and die, plunging the space into terrifying, claustrophobic shadows. The reinforced glass windows lock with deafening, synchronized metallic CLACKS. The architecture literally groans, the walls seemingly pressing inward as the house flexes its muscles.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Sensory deprivation and claustrophobia. The house exerts its absolute power over the environment, isolating Valerie entirely.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: The hardwood floorboards groan and violently buckle, tilting upward at a steep forty-five-degree angle. Valerie loses her footing, sliding down the slick wood. The center of the floor peels back like rotten skin, revealing a massive, fleshy maw lined with jagged wooden teeth. It is twice the size it was before, gnashing eagerly.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The Climax of the Sequence: The physical manifestation of the monster's insatiable hunger, putting Valerie in imminent, lethal danger.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie claws frantically at the floorboards, her expensive manicured nails breaking and bleeding as she fights gravity. Her corporate facade is entirely gone, replaced by feral, screaming panic. Dangling inches above the snapping jaws, she screams out a desperate bargain: She promises a gala. A massive, invite-only buffet of the city's absolute worst elites. A feast.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Character break: Valerie abandons all pretense and trades mass murder for her own survival, fully committing to her moral bankruptcy.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - LATER
scenes.narrative_action: The gnashing teeth freeze. The floorboards slowly, mechanically lower back to a perfectly level, pristine state. The bloody walls clear. The front door unlocks with a soft, polite click. Valerie collapses onto her back, chest heaving, staring up at the vaulted ceiling. She realizes with chilling certainty: she is merely the monster's captive pet.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Resolution & Pivot: Establishes the new status quo and sets the narrative engine for Act II's second half (planning the deadly gala).

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie, wearing a stunning, blood-red crimson gown, glides through a packed, glittering gala. She orchestrates the room like a battlefield commander, physically guiding corrupt politicians and white-collar criminals toward the darkest, most cavernous rooms. Richard Sterling stands by the fireplace, sipping scotch and nodding approvingly at her ruthless efficiency. Valerie's smile is razor-sharp, but her eyes dart nervously to the floorboards, which vibrate faintly beneath her designer stilettos.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes Valerie's false sense of control and sets the stage for the mass feeding, placing all major players inside the trap.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - MASTER SUITE - NIGHT
scenes.narrative_action: Julian Vance slips into the restricted master bedroom, locking the door behind him. Sweating and twitchy, he snorts a bump of cocaine off a pristine glass nightstand. He rips open Valerie's locked desk drawers with a letter opener, frantically digging until he pulls out a thick folder. He flips through missing persons reports and property anomalies, his unearned bravado morphing into a malicious, triumphant grin.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Introduces the immediate external threat to Valerie's empire. Julian discovers the truth, arming the ticking time bomb of exposure.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - DIM HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie marches down a secluded corridor, carrying a tray of champagne. Julian steps out of the shadows, blocking her path. He shoves the stolen folder into her chest, his breath reeking of gin. He aggressively mocks her 'lucky vibes' and demands a fifty-percent cut of her illicit operation, threatening to livestream the documents to his followers right now. Valerie's polished veneer finally cracks; she steps back, her eyes wide with feral panic.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The confrontation. Julian corners the protagonist, stripping away her transactional defenses and forcing her into a desperate corner.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - DIM HALLWAY / LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Before Valerie can answer, the ambient recessed lighting flickers and violently shifts to a sickly, pulsating crimson. Julian looks up, confused. The pristine white drywall behind him suddenly ripples and softens like bruised flesh. A synchronized, ear-splitting barrage of metallic SLAMS echoes through the estate. Every single exit—the mahogany front doors, the patio glass, the windows—seals shut with thick, bone-like deadbolts. Julian drops his phone in terror.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The midpoint of the sequence. The House loses patience with Valerie's timeline, dropping its disguise and trapping everyone inside.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Absolute chaos erupts. The modernist masterpiece devolves into a fleshy labyrinth. Screaming guests stampede toward the sealed doors. A massive, white-leather sectional sofa abruptly liquefies into a gaping, toothy sinkhole, swallowing a group of embezzling tech billionaires whole. Valerie strides through the blood-soaked carnage, dodging snapping floorboards with a terrifying, bulletproof arrogance. She ignores the screams, assuming her status as the 'provider' grants her absolute immunity.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Showcases the sheer scale of the monster's power while highlighting Valerie's sociopathic hubris as she watches her enemies die.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - DINING AREA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie rounds the corner and freezes. Richard Sterling stands perfectly still, calmly observing the slaughter. The house's fleshy tendrils whip right past him, completely ignoring the apex predator. Instead, the tendrils snatch up the terrified, underpaid catering staff. The innocent servers are dragged screaming into the ceiling vents, their silver trays of hors d'oeuvres clattering against the blood-stained marble. Valerie's arrogant smirk drops into absolute horror.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The horrific pivot. The monster breaks its own rules, proving its hunger is indiscriminate and shattering Valerie's moral justification.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - EAST CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Toby, clutching his affordable housing pitch deck to his chest, scrambles backward down a rapidly narrowing hallway. The drywall has calcified into jagged, grinding plates that are pressing inward. He nervously adjusts his glasses, hyperventilating as the walls physically crush the blueprints in his hands. He hits a dead end, the fleshy walls closing in to crush him flat.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Raises the stakes to their absolute peak by putting Valerie's only moral anchor in imminent, lethal danger.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - EAST CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie abandons all pretense of composure. She kicks off her stilettos and dives into the shifting, fleshy corridor. She reaches into the grinding gap, grabbing Toby by the collar of his cheap suit. With a guttural scream of exertion, she yanks him backward just as the calcified walls slam together with a deafening crack, obliterating Toby's pitch deck.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Action-driven character growth. Valerie risks her own physical safety to save someone else, taking her first true selfless action.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drags a coughing, terrified Toby out of the corridor and shoves him toward the grand floating glass staircase. They scramble upward, but the house violently reacts. The glass steps buckle and shatter under their feet. Valerie and Toby are thrown forward, barely catching themselves on a narrow, trembling mid-stair landing. The staircase below them collapses entirely into the foundation.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Cuts off the escape route, physically isolating the protagonist and her B-story anchor for the final confrontation of the sequence.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - STAIRCASE LANDING - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Beneath the landing, the polished hardwood floor splits wide open. A massive, acidic maw lined with rows of translucent, jagged teeth breaches the surface. A suffocating stench of sulfur and digested gore billows up. A deep, guttural vibration rattles through Valerie's skull—a telepathic demand from the entity. The house pulses with dark energy, the landing tilting slightly, silently commanding Valerie to push Toby into the pit to secure her own survival and wealth.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The ultimate test of the protagonist's flaw. The antagonist (the house) offers Valerie exactly what she wants, for the price of her soul.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - STAIRCASE LANDING - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie grips the trembling railing. She looks down at the churning acid, then over to Toby. Toby is weeping, his hands trembling as he grips the fabric of her crimson gown like a frightened child. Valerie stares into his terrified eyes and sees her own reflection. She looks across the cavernous room and locks eyes with Richard Sterling, who is watching her with a cold, approving nod. Valerie's breath catches. She grabs Toby's hand tightly, anchoring him to her, and glares down into the maw, refusing the house's deal.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The emotional climax of the sequence. Valerie recognizes her own monstrous reflection, rejects her false belief, and chooses humanity over capitalism.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie, wearing a stunning crimson gown, glides through a packed, glittering gala. She orchestrates the room like a battlefield commander, physically guiding corrupt politicians and white-collar criminals toward the darkest, most cavernous rooms. Richard Sterling stands by the fireplace, sipping scotch and nodding approvingly at her ruthless efficiency. Valerie's smile is razor-sharp, but her eyes dart nervously to the floorboards, which vibrate faintly beneath her designer stilettos.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Establishes Valerie's false sense of control and sets the stage for the mass feeding, placing all major players inside the trap.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - MASTER SUITE - NIGHT
scenes.narrative_action: Julian Vance slips into the restricted master bedroom, locking the door behind him. Sweating and twitchy, he snorts a bump of cocaine off a pristine glass nightstand. He rips open Valerie's locked desk drawers with a letter opener, frantically digging until he pulls out a thick folder. He flips through missing persons reports and property anomalies, his unearned bravado morphing into a malicious, triumphant grin.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Introduces the immediate external threat to Valerie's empire. Julian discovers the truth, arming the ticking time bomb of exposure.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - DIM HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie marches down a secluded corridor, carrying a tray of champagne. Julian steps out of the shadows, blocking her path. He shoves the stolen folder into her chest, his breath reeking of gin. He aggressively mocks her 'lucky vibes' and demands a fifty-percent cut of her illicit operation, threatening to livestream the documents to his followers right now. Valerie's polished veneer finally cracks; she steps back, her eyes wide with feral panic.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The confrontation. Julian corners the protagonist, stripping away her transactional defenses and forcing her into a desperate corner.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - DIM HALLWAY / LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Before Valerie can answer, the ambient recessed lighting flickers and violently shifts to a sickly, pulsating crimson. Julian looks up, confused. The pristine white drywall behind him suddenly ripples and softens like bruised flesh. A synchronized, ear-splitting barrage of metallic SLAMS echoes through the estate. Every single exit—the mahogany front doors, the patio glass, the windows—seals shut with thick, bone-like deadbolts. Julian drops his phone in terror.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The midpoint of the sequence. The House loses patience with Valerie's timeline, dropping its disguise and trapping everyone inside.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Absolute chaos erupts. The modernist masterpiece devolves into a fleshy labyrinth. Screaming guests stampede toward the sealed doors. A massive, white-leather sectional sofa abruptly liquefies into a gaping, toothy sinkhole, swallowing a group of embezzling tech billionaires whole. Valerie strides through the blood-soaked carnage, dodging snapping floorboards with a terrifying, bulletproof arrogance. She ignores the screams, assuming her status as the 'provider' grants her absolute immunity.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Showcases the sheer scale of the monster's power while highlighting Valerie's sociopathic hubris as she watches her enemies die.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - DINING AREA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie rounds the corner and freezes. Richard Sterling stands perfectly still, calmly observing the slaughter. The house's fleshy tendrils whip right past him, completely ignoring the apex predator. Instead, the tendrils snatch up the terrified, underpaid catering staff. The innocent servers are dragged screaming into the ceiling vents, their silver trays of hors d'oeuvres clattering against the blood-stained marble. Valerie's arrogant smirk drops into absolute horror.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: The horrific pivot. The monster breaks its own rules, proving its hunger is indiscriminate and shattering Valerie's moral justification.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - EAST CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Toby, clutching his affordable housing pitch deck to his chest, scrambles backward down a rapidly narrowing hallway. The drywall has calcified into jagged, grinding plates that are pressing inward. He nervously adjusts his glasses, hyperventilating as the walls physically crush the blueprints in his hands. He hits a dead end, the fleshy walls closing in to crush him flat.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Raises the stakes to their absolute peak by putting Valerie's only moral anchor in imminent, lethal danger.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - EAST CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie abandons all pretense of composure. She kicks off her stilettos and dives into the shifting, fleshy corridor. She reaches into the grinding gap, grabbing Toby by the collar of his cheap suit. With a guttural scream of exertion, she yanks him backward just as the calcified walls slam together with a deafening crack, obliterating Toby's pitch deck.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Action-driven character growth. Valerie risks her own physical safety to save someone else, taking her first true selfless action.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - GRAND STAIRCASE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Valerie drags a coughing, terrified Toby out of the corridor and shoves him toward the grand floating glass staircase. They scramble upward, but the house violently reacts. The glass steps buckle and shatter under their feet. Valerie and Toby are thrown forward, barely catching themselves on a narrow, trembling mid-stair landing. The staircase below them collapses entirely into the foundation.
scenes.dramaturgical_function: Cuts off the escape route, physically isolating the protagonist and her B-story anchor for the final confrontation of the sequence.

scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - STAIRCASE LANDING - CONTINUOUS
scenes.narrative_action: Beneath the landing, the polished hardwood floor splits wide open. A massive, acidic maw lined with rows of translucent, jagged teeth breaches the surface. scenes.scene_heading: INT. MODERNIST MANSION - STAIRCASE LANDING - CONTINUOUS
